classObjective: Construct and interpret histograms and box plots to visualize the shape of a data distribution.
workedExamples: 9-5, Example 1 — Construct Histograms and Box Plots
9-5, Example 2 — Describe Center and Spread Using a Histogram
warmUp: Describe the shape of a histogram that is highest in the middle and tails off equally on both sides
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: symmetric, skewed left, skewed right distributions; constructing and interpreting histograms and box plots
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice constructing histograms and box plots and describing distribution shapes
independentPractice: Practice problems constructing and interpreting histograms and box plots
assessment: Exit ticket: given a histogram, describe the shape (symmetric, skewed left/right) and what this tells us about the data
capReflection: How does the shape of a distribution help you choose which measures of center and spread to use?
